description: Phase 3 of the gap-analysis program (backlog #347): each extracted capability (#346) joined to a Web Everything entity and labelled covered / partial / missing / native-covered, weighed native-first. Partial/missing rows carry the gap-# triage (nativeFirst / gap / effort / rank) and an alreadyTracked flag (deduped against the backlog). The `gaps` array — un-tracked partial/missing rows — is the direct input to phase 4 (#348), which files them as candidate backlog items.

labels.covered: A WE entity (or a native primitive WE defers to) fully provides the capability.
labels.native-covered: The web platform provides it natively and WE intentionally defers to the native primitive (native-first).
labels.partial: A WE entity addresses the capability but misses dimensions, states, or an implementation block.
labels.missing: No WE entity and no native primitive covers it.

method.join: Each capability id resolved against blocks/intents/plugs/protocols/intents and the native platform. nativeAnchorHint from #346 promoted to the native-first weighing: a capability the platform provides natively is NOT a gap even if no WE block wraps it.
method.dedup: Before a row is called a fileable gap it is reconciled against existing backlog items (open + parked). alreadyTracked records the #NNN so phase 4 does not re-open settled work.
method.scope: Coverage labels reflect the first-pass capability matrix (#346); they refine as per-source extraction (#352) sharpens the matrix.

summary.note: fileableGaps: 11 at phase-3 -> 7 at phase-4 file-time dedup (menu+context-menu #173/#300, command-palette #299/#016, accordion #008) -> 6 when fill-time review found range-slider already covered by block:slider's dual-thumb range variant (#361). The sweep self-corrects: dedup and fill-verification both demote false gaps.

coveredHighlights.we: block:dropdown / droplist (+ native select, Open UI selectlist)
block:drawer (concrete member of the dialog family, #360)
block:notification (implements feedback intent; toast is a presentation style, #358)
block:slider (dual-thumb range — already specified; impl is Frontier UI's, #361)

alreadyTracked.note: dropdown async pagination paradigm tracked in #018.
DnD paradigms tracked in #022; intents cover the axis.
menu/menubar block tracked in #173 + #300 (standard); context-menu in #300 too.
context-menu folded into the menu/menubar/context-menu block standard #300.
command intent + invocation tracked in #299 / webcommands #016.
disclosure intent (gap-12) tracked in #008; accordion is a thin composition over it + native details.

gaps.gapNote: intent:temporal defines the UX, but no calendar / date / time / range picker block exists; native input[type=date] is the anchor for the simple case.
No unified design-token / theming system (spacing, radius, elevation, semantic color roles, light/dark). Several intents touch slices; relates to theme-color #010.

skippedReason: Low value, a pure styling idiom, native-covered, folded into a larger filed gap (spacing/radius/elevation -> design-tokens-theming; contrast -> #010; tree-view -> #064), or composed trivially from existing entities. Logged here rather than filed so the omission is explicit, not silent.
